title: The Prime of Miss Jean Brodie
author: Muriel Spark

setting.note: In 1930s Edinburgh, the charismatic and dangerous schoolteacher Jean Brodie grooms her chosen set of six girls in art, romance and her own admiration for Mussolini, until one of them betrays her. Spark's city is her own: the genteel girls' school of the south side, the walk Brodie leads through the poverty of the Old Town, the Links and Meadows where the set parades, and the village of Cramond on the Forth where the music master lives.

palette.note: The red of Jean Brodie's passion and of the fascism she half-worships, the danger under the genteel Edinburgh surface. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
